heaviest_rainfall.status: Today's storm ranked 7th for total rainfall with 19.13mm having fallen, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  5.4mm per hour fell at points during the storm which lasted 19.9 hours.  47.89mm of rain was recorded as having fallen on 09 November 2025, marking an exceptional 144.8 hour precipitation event.  At times the rain was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is in the area.  This is the first such event since 29 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 6 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (197°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 7.0°C), outside temperature (up 6.3°C), and inside temperature (up 1.8°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 8%) and rainfall (up 2.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 501 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in rainfall (down 1.6 mm), cloud levels (up 327 m), feels like temperature (down 2.5°C), and outside temperature (down 2.5°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 501.4m lower than yesterday, 275.8m lower than the past week, 326.5m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 854m minimum and 1568m maximum, with an average of 614.5m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 890.3m and 941.0m.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; cold temperatures are forecasted. Also, air moisture should feel ok and very little to no wind will be present. Rain showers likely, overcast skies with low cloud, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The current weather conditions are not suitable as it is raining.
